text:
Market today: Chips are ripping today — Micron's blowout earnings are lifting the whole semiconductor space. That's your tailwind for SMH and AVGO, but core inflation hitting 3.4% (highest since October) is a real headwind since it pushes rate cuts further out, and high-rate environments compress tech multiples.

Your holdings: PLTR is your biggest problem — down 17.55% and on a SELL signal; on margin, that hole gets deeper fast, and your stop at $85 is still well below current price so you have room to act before it triggers. AVGO is your largest position, down 14.25% on a TRIM signal — today's chip rally might give you a better exit price, don't waste it. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no trading history — margin amplifies any sudden moves there, keep size small.

Watch: (1) How AVGO specifically trades into the close on this chip rally — trim into strength, not weakness. (2) Whether the inflation print starts pulling rate-sensitive tech lower by afternoon.

Bottom line: Use today's chip rally as your window to trim AVGO and cut or reduce PLTR before the momentum fades.
